sections.markdown:
# DEFCLASS Basics

The Common Lisp Object System (CLOS) is class-based but unusual. Classes describe **slots** (data) only; behaviour lives in **generic functions** (next lesson). A class definition looks like:

```lisp
(defclass point ()
  ((x :initarg :x :accessor point-x :initform 0 :type real)
   (y :initarg :y :accessor point-y :initform 0 :type real)))
```

The parts:

* `()` is the **superclass list**. Empty here; default is `(standard-object)`.
* Each slot is `(slot-name option*)`. Common options:
  * `:initarg :keyword` — name accepted by `make-instance`.
  * `:initform expr` — default value if no initarg given. **Re-evaluated** for each new instance.
  * `:accessor name` — auto-define a generic function reading the slot AND a `(setf name)` method to write it.
  * `:reader name` — read-only accessor.
  * `:writer name` — write-only accessor; called as `(setf (name obj) v)` won't work — use `(funcall (function (setf name)) v obj)` or just use `:accessor`.
  * `:type type-spec` — declared element type. Implementations may or may not check it.
  * `:allocation :instance` (default) or `:class` (one slot shared by all instances).

To create an instance: `(make-instance 'point :x 3 :y 4)`. To read by accessor: `(point-x p)`. To read raw: `(slot-value p 'x)`.

If you read a slot that was never initialised and has no `:initform`, `slot-value` signals `unbound-slot`. Accessors signal the same. So either set an initform, pass an initarg, or be ready to handle the condition.

sections.explain: MAKE-INSTANCE accepts initargs as keyword arguments; the auto-defined accessor reads the slot.
Y wasn't passed; its INITFORM 0 is used.
:ALLOCATION :CLASS shares the slot across all instances. Bumping A's N is visible to B.
SLOT-VALUE works without an accessor, but using accessors is the idiomatic interface.

sections.prompt: Define a class CIRCLE with one slot RADIUS (initarg :radius, accessor circle-radius, initform 1). Define DEMO-RADIUS that returns the radius of (make-instance 'circle :radius 5).
sections.hint: Replace the (defclass ... ()) with a slot, and DEMO-RADIUS returns (circle-radius (make-instance 'circle :radius 5)).
